author: isanghao
title: Windows VS 2022 workflow logs unavailable - run re-queued automatically on attempt 2
signature: Windows VS2022 workflow run failed - job logs unavailable due to secrecy policy|Infrastructure

failed_jobs.name: Windows (VS 2022, Python 3.11, Release)
failed_jobs.symptom: Run failed on attempt 1; attempt 2 was automatically queued. Job logs inaccessible due to secrecy policy.

key_errors: No log data accessible: filtered by secrecy policy (resource:get_job_logs). Pre-downloaded logs contain no job entries.
root_cause: The root cause cannot be determined because job logs are inaccessible via the secrecy policy filter. The workflow run shows run_attempt=2 and status=queued, indicating the system already re-queued the run after the attempt 1 failure. This pattern is consistent with a transient infrastructure issue (runner hiccup, resource exhaustion, or scheduling issue) that triggered an automatic retry.
reproduction_steps: N/A - logs not available for reproduction analysis
recommended_actions: Monitor attempt 2 outcome to determine if the failure was transient
If attempt 2 also fails, manually inspect job logs via the GitHub Actions UI for detailed error information
Check if other Windows VS2022 runs failed around the same time to identify a systemic issue
prevention_strategies: No specific prevention strategies can be recommended without log data. Ensure GitHub Actions runner capacity is sufficient for Windows builds.
historical_context: No matching pattern found in the investigation database for this specific failure signature. The run was already re-queued automatically (attempt 2 is queued), suggesting the system or a prior CI Doctor run identified this as transient.
